chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Bail Appl.No.618 of 2026 Date Order with signature of the Judge Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro. Mr. Justice Khalid Hussain Shahani. Inayatullah..............Vs........................ The State 01.04.2026. Mr. Muhammad Rafique Brohi, Advocate for applicant Ms. Rubina Qadir, DPG. a/w I.O. O R D E R = MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: A police team of P.S. Mauripur on snap checking arrested applicant while driving a Rickshaw on 26.01.2026 from Chatara Bypass Mubarak Village alongwith 03 others accused, duly named in the FIR, on finding them suspicious and recovered 9 K.G. Charas from the Rickshaw. From applicant individually 2.3 K.G. charas was recovered, hence they all were booked in FIR bearing Cr. No.20/2026 U/s 9 3(c), Sindh, CNS Act, 2025 of P.S. Mauripur, Karachi. 2. Learned counsel for applicant has pleaded for bail on the ground that accused is presumed to be innocent unless proved otherwise; the case is not punishable for death, hence applicant is entitled to bail; no video recording of incident has been done, hence the case requires further inquiry. 3. On the other hand, learned D.P.G. has opposed the bail by pointing to recovery of huge quantity of Charas and has rebutted argument of learned defence counsel by stating that video recording of the incident has been done in this case. 4. We have considered pleas of the respective parties and are of the view that prima facie there are reasonable grounds to believe that applicant is involved in recovery of 9 K.G Charas found in Rickshaw which was being driven by him. From him individually, more than 2 K.Gs of Charas was recovered.

We have considered pleas of the respective parties and are of the view that prima facie there are reasonable grounds to believe that applicant is involved in recovery of 9 K.G Charas found in Rickshaw which was being driven by him. From him individually, more than 2 K.Gs of Charas was recovered. Such recovery is supported by statements of witnesses u/s 161 Cr.P.C as well as video recording of the incident. No case for bail is made out. Accordingly, this bail application is dismissed. However, learned trial court is directed to expedite the trial and conclude it within a reasonable time. The Cr. Bail Application is disposed of. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE 2 A.K
